titles.en: Chapter 118 – The loan which attracts the benefit
Chapter 118 – The loan which attracts the benefit

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَسْتَقْرِضُ مِنَ الرَّجُلِ قَرْضاً وَ يُعْطِيهِ الرَّهْنَ إِمَّا خَادِماً وَ إِمَّا آنِيَةً وَ إِمَّا ثِيَاباً فَيَحْتَاجُ إِلَى شَيْ‏ءٍ مِنْ مَنْفَعَتِهِ فَيَسْتَأْذِنُهُ فِيهِ فَيَأْذَنُ لَهُ قَالَ إِذَا طَابَتْ نَفْسُهُ فَلَا بَأْسَ قُلْتُ إِنَّ مَنْ عِنْدَنَا يَرْوُونَ أَنَّ كُلَّ قَرْضٍ يَجُرُّ مَنْفَعَةً فَهُوَ فَاسِدٌ فَقَالَ أَ وَ لَيْسَ خَيْرُ الْقَرْضِ مَا جَرَّ مَنْفَعَةً .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَسْتَقْرِضُ مِنَ الرَّجُلِ قَرْضاً وَ يُعْطِيهِ الرَّهْنَ إِمَّا خَادِماً وَ إِمَّا آنِيَةً وَ إِمَّا ثِيَاباً فَيَحْتَاجُ إِلَى شَيْ‏ءٍ مِنْ مَنْفَعَتِهِ فَيَسْتَأْذِنُهُ فِيهِ فَيَأْذَنُ لَهُ قَالَ إِذَا طَابَتْ نَفْسُهُ فَلَا بَأْسَ قُلْتُ إِنَّ مَنْ عِنْدَنَا يَرْوُونَ أَنَّ كُلَّ قَرْضٍ يَجُرُّ مَنْفَعَةً فَهُوَ فَاسِدٌ فَقَالَ أَ وَ لَيْسَ خَيْرُ الْقَرْضِ مَا جَرَّ مَنْفَعَةً .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Muslim and someone else who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the man who borrowed a loan from the man and gave him the security deposit, either a servant, or a utensil, or clothes. So he (lender) was needy to the thing (deposited as security) for its benefits. So he sought permission with regards to it, so he (the borrower) permitted him. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When he agreed himself, so there is no problem’. I said, ‘The ones among us are reporting that every loan which attracts benefits, so it is invalid’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Or is it not the case that the best of the loans is what attracts a benefit?’
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Muslim and someone else who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the man who borrowed a loan from the man and gave him the security deposit, either a servant, or a utensil, or clothes. So he (lender) was needy to the thing (deposited as security) for its benefits. So he sought permission with regards to it, so he (the borrower) permitted him. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When he agreed himself, so there is no problem’. I said, ‘The ones among us are reporting that every loan which attracts benefits, so it is invalid’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Or is it not the case that the best of the loans is what attracts a benefit?’

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim and others who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who asked for a loan. He left al-Rahn (security deposit) like a servant or utensils or cloths. The party needed to benefit from them, so he asked permission and he gave permission. He (the Imam) said, Tf permission is given without compulsion it is not unlawful/ I then said, Tn our area people see it as if every loan brings benefits. Is it invalid?’ He (the Imam) said, Ts it not a fact that the best loans are those which attract benefits?’”
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim and others who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who asked for a loan. He left al-Rahn (security deposit) like a servant or utensils or cloths. The party needed to benefit from them, so he asked permission and he gave permission. He (the Imam) said, Tf permission is given without compulsion it is not unlawful/ I then said, Tn our area people see it as if every loan brings benefits. Is it invalid?’ He (the Imam) said, Ts it not a fact that the best loans are those which attract benefits?’”

gradings.label: حسن
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/322)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ وَ غَيْرِهِ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 118 – The loan which attracts the benefit

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقَرْضِ يَجُرُّ الْمَنْفَعَةَ فَقَالَ خَيْرُ الْقَرْضِ الَّذِي يَجُرُّ الْمَنْفَعَةَ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقَرْضِ يَجُرُّ الْمَنْفَعَةَ فَقَالَ خَيْرُ الْقَرْضِ الَّذِي يَجُرُّ الْمَنْفَعَةَ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Safwan, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Muhammad Bin Abdat who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the loan which attracts the benefits. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The best of the loans is that which attracts the benefits’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Safwan, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Muhammad Bin Abdat who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the loan which attracts the benefits. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The best of the loans is that which attracts the benefits’.

segments: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Safwan from ibn Bukayr from Muhammad ibn ‘Abdah who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if loan attracted benefits. He (the Imam) said, ‘The best loan is that which attracts benefits.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Safwan from ibn Bukayr from Muhammad ibn ‘Abdah who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if loan attracted benefits. He (the Imam) said, ‘The best loan is that which attracts benefits.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/322)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/322)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدَةَ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 118 – The loan which attracts the benefit

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Bishr Bin Maslama, and someone else, from the one who informed him,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The best loan is what attracts the benefits’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Bishr Bin Maslama, and someone else, from the one who informed him,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The best loan is what attracts the benefits’.

segments: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Bishr ibn Muslimah from more than one person who reported who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The best loan is that which attracts benefits.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Bishr ibn Muslimah from more than one person who reported who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The best loan is that which attracts benefits.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/322)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/322)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بِشْرِ بْنِ مَسْلَمَةَ وَ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ عَمَّنْ أَخْبَرَهُمْ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter 118 – The loan which attracts the benefit

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَجِيئُنِي فَأَشْتَرِي لَهُ الْمَتَاعَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَ أَضْمَنُ عَنْهُ ثُمَّ يَجِيئُنِي بِالدَّرَاهِمِ فَآخُذُهَا وَ أَحْبِسُهَا عَنْ صَاحِبِهَا وَ آخُذُ الدَّرَاهِمَ الْجِيَادَ وَ أُعْطِي دُونَهَا فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَ يَضْمَنُ فَرُبَّمَا اشْتَدَّ عَلَيْهِ فَعَجَّلَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْخُذَهُ وَ يَحْبِسُ بَعْدَ مَا يَأْخُذُ فَلَا بَأْسَ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَجِيئُنِي فَأَشْتَرِي لَهُ الْمَتَاعَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَ أَضْمَنُ عَنْهُ ثُمَّ يَجِيئُنِي بِالدَّرَاهِمِ فَآخُذُهَا وَ أَحْبِسُهَا عَنْ صَاحِبِهَا وَ آخُذُ الدَّرَاهِمَ الْجِيَادَ وَ أُعْطِي دُونَهَا فَقَالَ إِذَا كَانَ يَضْمَنُ فَرُبَّمَا اشْتَدَّ عَلَيْهِ فَعَجَّلَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْخُذَهُ وَ يَحْبِسُ بَعْدَ مَا يَأْخُذُ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

segments: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from safwan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the man who came to me, so I bought goods for him from the people and I guaranteed on his behalf. Then he came to me with the Dirhams. I took and withheld them from their owner, then took the good quality Dirhams and gave them of lower quality. He<sup>asws</sup> (the Imam) said, ‘He guaranteed payment, perhaps it became severe upon him so he hurried to pay before he (the guarantor) asks for payment. He (the guarantor) withholds payment after receiving, it is not unlawful.’”.
en.hubeali.text:
Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from safwan, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about the man who came to me, so I bought goods for him from the people and I guaranteed on his behalf. Then he came to me with the Dirhams. I took and withheld them from their owner, then took the good quality Dirhams and gave them of lower quality. He<sup>asws</sup> (the Imam) said, ‘He guaranteed payment, perhaps it became severe upon him so he hurried to pay before he (the guarantor) asks for payment. He (the guarantor) withholds payment after receiving, it is not unlawful.’”.

segments: 4. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who came to me; I bought for him goods from people and guaranteed payment on his behalf. Then he came to me with dirham. I took and withheld them from their owner, then took the good quality dirhams and gave them of lower quality. He (the Imam) said, ‘He guaranteed payment, perhaps it became severe upon him so he hurried to pay before he (the guarantor) asks for payment. He (the guarantor) withholds payment after receiving, it is not unlawful.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who came to me; I bought for him goods from people and guaranteed payment on his behalf. Then he came to me with dirham. I took and withheld them from their owner, then took the good quality dirhams and gave them of lower quality. He (the Imam) said, ‘He guaranteed payment, perhaps it became severe upon him so he hurried to pay before he (the guarantor) asks for payment. He (the guarantor) withholds payment after receiving, it is not unlawful.’”

narratorChain.raw: أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الْأَشْعَرِيُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ
